title: erica stagnalis subsp. minor
intro: Erica stagnalis subsp. minor, also known as Cross-leaved Heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Erica stagnalis subsp. minor include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used internally to support respiratory health and externally to soothe skin irritations.
